metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1685
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This paper shows data on in utero electroporation of TUBB5 (orthologue of human TUBB) shRNA in the mouse brain. Neuronal migration in these animals is impaired. On the synapse level, Fig 5 shows that also the number of spines on apical and basal dendrites is reduced (Fig5A-C), and that the type of spine in more of the immature "thin" type, rather then the mature "mushroom" type (Fig5D-E). The same result is observed when disease mutant TUBB5-M299V are overexpressed (Fig 6).

2/11/2017 Pim
- 'regulation of' selected because the effect of TUBB perturbation is rather upstream of maturing the synapses (transport, stability of anchorpoints, etc.). Experimental description: M&M: "Mice were maintained within the animal facilities at Monash University."
